title: Postdoctoral Researcher in Migration Studies — ERC Consolidator Grant, US and Latin America
provider: IMISCOE Jobs & Opportunities / University for Continuing Education Krems
summary: Three-year postdoctoral researcher position at the University for Continuing Education Krems within an ERC project on ethical dilemmas of migrant solidarity. The postdoc will lead a work package on sanctuary/solidarity cities in the United States and Latin America, using qualitative fieldwork and contributing to theory-building on migration governance and solidarity.
target_group: Postdoctoral researcher with completed PhD by 15 October 2026 in political science, sociology, public policy, urban studies, migration studies or related field; strong publication record in migration; expertise in urban and multi-level migration governance; comparative research design; qualitative methods; English C1; Spanish and/or Portuguese C1; willingness to relocate to Krems, Vienna or surrounding areas.

title: HORIZON-CL2-2026-01-TRANSFO-08 — Strengthened implementation of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum and a focus on inclusion, integration, and health
summary: Horizon Europe research funding topic on the implementation of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum, with a focus on evidence-based migration governance, asylum management, age assessment of minors, resettlement, legal pathways, migrant and refugee health data, and long-term social inclusion and integration strategies.
target_group: Research and Innovation Action under Horizon Europe. Typically suitable for universities, research centres, public bodies, NGOs, international organisations and other legal entities participating in a consortium. Exact eligibility and consortium rules must be checked in the official Funding & Tenders Portal topic conditions.

title: Luxembourg subsidies for intercultural projects supporting third-country national integration
provider: European Commission / Luxembourg Ministry of Family, Solidarity, Living Together and Hospitality
summary: Luxembourg funding scheme for non-profit organisations, foundations and social impact entities developing projects that support intercultural living together, anti-racism, equal opportunities and the integration of third-country nationals. Applications can be submitted during the year, with a key deadline on 8 October 2026.
target_group: Non-profit organisations, foundations and social impact entities initiating projects that promote intercultural living together, equal opportunities, anti-racism, anti-discrimination and integration of third-country nationals in Luxembourg.

title: Call for Proposals - Migration Partnerships
summary: Rolling call under the Migration Partnership Facility supporting EU migration priorities with partner countries and projects aligned with EU Talent Partnerships. It can fund migration governance, legal/labour mobility, migrant protection, asylum policy, reintegration, diaspora-related activities and capacity-building. It is highly relevant but administratively complex and not designed for small informal organisations.
target_group: Legal persons. Lead applicants may include EU Member State public bodies, entities with an official mandate on behalf of an EU Member State public body, and non-profit private or public law bodies registered in an EU Member State, including NGOs, foundations, private companies and universities. Co-applicants may include public bodies, international organisations, NGOs and other private law bodies in EU or partner countries.

title: Making Europe a global magnet for talent - Attracting and retaining students, researchers and high-skilled workers from outside the EU
summary: Horizon Europe research funding topic on attracting and retaining students, researchers and high-skilled workers from outside the EU. The topic is strongly migration-relevant but primarily suited to research consortia rather than small organisations applying alone.
target_group: Research and innovation consortia; exact eligibility to be checked in Horizon Europe General Annex B and topic conditions.
